replies.msg: 又一例證

如果你問讀音嘅話你不如算鳩數啦
但如果你問佢地點分邊隻用法，其實佢地都係靠sense即時判斷語境。例如一份功課上面寫著read，正常就係指已閱，咁就肯定係pp嘅形態。你諗下點會就咁present tense 嘅read一隻字攤落嚟，唔通寫個個人叫你「睇」咩？
如果係句子入面嘅read都係同樣原理，睇上文下理去分佢咩tense就算 “set”所有tense嘅讀音一樣都係咁分

If u want to ask about the written form, sometimes there will be other implications for u to decide whether it is present tense or past tense. replies.msg:
呢個用法上要被動式因為你係「被設想」去做乜乜乜，

英文DSE 1分

如果用返樓主個例子

What were you supposed to do yesterday?

同埋大家既討論都可以見到
時間性未必剩係可以反映係Tenses 到
例如
Read a book (睇一本書)
Read the book (睇果本書，即係本書你已經入手一段時間)
都已經有唔同意思

同埋根據情況 時間上既野都會影響 word choice
例如睇完會用 Finish the book, 而唔係 read'ed' the book

另外鬼佬對於呢啲 Present tense 同 past tense 形態一樣既字佢地會有種共識會用present perfect tense 去表達

以上有錯請糾正

想read就read 想read就read

quote.msg:
如果用返樓主個例子

What were you supposed to do yesterday?

同埋大家既討論都可以見到
時間性未必剩係可以反映係Tenses 到
例如
Read a book (睇一本書)
Read the book (睇果本書，即係本書你已經入手一段時間)
都已經有唔同意思

同埋根據情況 時間上既野都會影響 word choice
例如睇完會用 Finish the book, 而唔係 read'ed' the book

另外鬼佬對於呢啲 Present tense 同 past tense 形態一樣既字佢地會有種共識會用present perfect tense 去表達

以上有錯請糾正

replies.msg:
Thanks for ur sharing. It is good that u have the example 'a' and 'the'. The difference between these two words is that 'a' is not specific, it can be any book. 'the' is more specific. It just can be the specific book u have in mind.
For the tense part, present perfect tense is totally different tense from the other 2 tenses. U can't mix using them. People will give other hints instead of using present perfect tense to distinguish whether it is present or past tense.
And for those who may concern, tense is one way to distinguish time, but it is not the only way

replies.msg: “Read” (pronounced “RED”) is both the past tense and past participle of “read” (pronounced “REED”). This is an irregular verb. Only the past tense and past participle are pronounced “RED” (short E); the other tenses are a long E (“REED”).

因為答緊 what you were supposed to do
“to do” 所以read 唔會係past tense, 應該係 to-infinitive （我諗應該係present infinitive)
I was supposed to read a book
短句： to read a book/read a book

“The infinitive is the base form of a verb. In English, when we talk about the infinitive we are usually referring to the present infinitive, which is the most common.”
